channel.title: Shreyas Korad — Writing
channel.description: Articles and insights on learning design, gamification, and L&D strategy.

item.title: Checklist for customer support pods: 6 moves to ship faster with less rework
item.description: Behavior transfer under real workload for customer support pods with a concrete test around manager coaching follow-through, a real workflow checkpoint, and a clear next action.

item.title: How to reduce execution drift without adding process overhead
item.description: Execution-centered weekly learning loop for managers with a concrete test around first-pass quality, a real workflow checkpoint, and a clear next action.

item.title: For real workflows: one checkpoint, one metric, one review rhythm
item.description: Execution-centered weekly learning loop for managers with a concrete test around decision turnaround time, a real workflow checkpoint, and a clear next action.

item.title: The AI learning loop: from content volume to decision quality
item.description: The AI learning loop: from content volume to decision quality is often treated as a writing problem, but the root issue is workflow design. When drafting, review, and measurement are disconnected, teams publish polished language that sounds complete yet fails to improve practical decision quality.

item.title: Taste, taste, and taste
item.description: Taste, taste, and taste discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment. The fastest teams are not the busiest; they are the ones making clearer decisions.

item.title: Prompt-driven learning for high-pressure decision making
item.description: Prompt-driven learning for high-pressure decision making discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment. What if learning improved this week’s decisions instead of next quarter’s compliance scores?

item.title: Operational patterns for l&d decisions under pressure (cycle 777)
item.description: A practical perspective on write one publish-ready article in your authentic focus areas: l&d, gamification, lxd, data-i with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workpl. Most friction is not missing information, it is unclear ownership during a recurring decision moment.

item.title: Manager-led learning loops that sharpen team judgment edition 906
item.description: A practical perspective on decision quality in workplace learning workflows (2230) with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workplace outcomes. Most friction is not missing information, it is unclear ownership during a recurring decision moment.

item.title: Reviews focused on behavior shifts not completion dashboards
item.description: Lxd reviews focused on behavior shifts not completion dashboards discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment. Execution rarely fails from effort; it fails from weak decisions made under pressure.

item.title: Judgment-enhanced learning without course fatigue
item.description: Judgment-enhanced learning without course fatigue discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment. Most teams optimize for speed first, then pay for it in rework later.

item.title: How small teams can implement data-informed learning decisions without heavy infrastructure
item.description: When drafting, review, and measurement are disconnected, teams publish polished language that sounds complete yet fails to improve practical decision quality. Teams frequently optimize for output speed without defining what behavior should change after publication.

item.title: From completion metrics to decision signals in workplace learning (772)
item.description: A practical perspective on write one publish-ready article in your authentic focus areas: l&d, gamification, lxd, data-i with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workpl. Most friction is not missing information, it is unclear ownership during a recurring decision moment.

item.title: Feedback rituals that actually change team behavior
item.description: Feedback rituals that actually change team behavior discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment. Most teams optimize for speed first, then pay for it in rework later.

item.title: Experiential Judgment Drills As The Primary Learning Method Instead Of Traditional Courses
item.description: Most professional training programs struggle to transfer knowledge into on the job performance because they rely on passive content consumption rather than active decision practice. Employees attend courses yet show minimal behavior change when faced with real workplace challenges.

item.title: Execution-first learning design for faster team judgment (version 776)
item.description: A practical perspective on design learning prompts that improve decisions during real project pressure with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workplace outcomes. In a weekly manager review, I often see teams discuss activity counts while skipping the decision that created avoidable rework.

item.title: Designing manager-led learning loops that change decisions (edition 775)
item.description: A practical perspective on build feedback rituals that convert learning into visible team behavior with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workplace outcomes. In a weekly manager review, I often see teams discuss activity counts while skipping the decision that created avoidable rework.

item.title: Decision quality in workplace learning workflows edition 904
item.description: A practical perspective on decision quality in workplace learning workflows (2230) with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workplace outcomes. Most friction is not missing information, it is unclear ownership during a recurring decision moment.

item.title: Create L&D reviews that focus on behavior shifts not completion dashboards
item.description: Stop measuring learning completion rates and start measuring behavior shifts that drive performance. Learn how to redesign L&D reviews for real learning impact.

item.title: Build practical lxd workflow for small teams shipping weekly
item.description: Most teams optimize for speed first, then pay for it in rework later. By weaving AI‑driven prompts directly into tools, teams receive instant feedback that sharpens judgment and reduces error rates.

item.title: Build feedback rituals that convert learning into visible team behavior
item.description: Build feedback rituals that convert learning into visible team behavior by bridging learning transfer principles and team behavior change through structured feedback rituals. Teams often complete training but nothing changes.

item.title: Powered learning feedback loops for managers
item.description: AI changes this by analyzing actual learning behaviors to surface personalized insights managers would never see otherwise. Ai-powered learning feedback loops for managers discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment.

item.title: Practical ai support for learning decisions in real workflows (issue 773)
item.description: A practical perspective on design manager enablement that links coaching actions to team capability growth with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workplace outcomes.

item.title: How teams improve decision quality in learning workflows (playbook 774)
item.description: A practical perspective on create l&d reviews that focus on behavior shifts not completion dashboards with evidence-driven coaching actions and measurable workplace outcomes.

item.title: Design manager enablement that links coaching actions to team capability growth
item.description: Connect manager coaching actions directly to observable team capability growth instead of abstract development goals. Learn practical frameworks for linking coaching to team skills.

item.title: Design learning prompts that improve decisions during real project pressure
item.description: Teams who practice with realistic prompts make better decisions when stakes are high. Studies show repeated exposure to pressure simulations strengthens decision muscles more effectively than classroom scenarios.

item.title: Data-Informed Coaching: A Manager's Practical Framework
item.description: Data-informed coaching helps managers improve team performance through simple metrics and regular check-ins.

item.description: Practical guidance on data and analytics with one operating model, one execution example, and a measurable review step.

item.title: A practical weekly loop leaders can run without heavy tooling (03-1046)
item.description: Practical approach for data and analytics using behavior science and analytics with measurable outcomes.

item.title: How to instrument decisions, not just completions (02-0957)
item.description: Practical approach for modern L&D using behavior science and analytics with measurable outcomes.

item.title: Practical decision system for measurable execution outcomes
item.description: Practical execution ideas on gamified learning, modern L&D, data and analytics, and behaviour science for leaders building measurable learning outcomes.

item.title: We are becoming too used to the shortest feedback loop
item.description: We are becoming too used to the shortest feedback loop discusses data‑backed strategies for embedding learning in daily workflows, boosting performance and judgment.

item.title: Workflow metrics for L&D teams
item.description: AI workflow metrics for L&D teams is most credible when it improves how teams decide, align, and follow through in everyday execution.

item.title: Assisted learning design must preserve human judgment
item.description: Learning design teams should treat AI as a decision amplifier not a decision-maker for effective learning design. First, keep humans in the loop for all critical decisions.

item.title: How managers coach decision quality without courses
item.description: Effective judgment coaching happens in real conversations, not slide decks. Managers should ask one question after every team decision to build decision muscle.

item.title: Building learning rituals into existing workflow meetings
item.description: Embed learning into existing team rhythms to scale knowledge without adding meetings. Repeated exposure across contexts strengthens memory retention compared to one-time sessions.

item.title: Use learning data to surface team blind spots not just completion metrics
item.description: This short format keeps use learning data to surface team blind spots not just completion metrics evidence based.

item.title: Build a practical LXD workflow for small teams shipping weekly
item.description: Build a practical LXD workflow for small teams shipping weekly gets real traction when teams tie it to one recurring decision and review what changed in the next cycle.
